tasks.description: Two real bugs observed in the 2026-07-04 DSM-1 dispatch attempts: (1) 'git pull --rebase' fails with exit 128 when the working tree has unstaged changes from the finalizer run — needs --autostash or an explicit stash/reset before rebase. (2) Multiple concurrent autopilot attempts race on writing governance/roadmap/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json, causing rejected pushes ('fetch first'). Needs either a retry-with-rebase-on-push loop or serialization (e.g. a lock file / concurrency group) so parallel runs don't collide.
acceptance_criteria: GPT: identify and report why its read of blob 8c06e59c3c00a56d3f20d29e09d851b640918205 differs from three independent EXTERNAL_AUDITOR_CLAUDE reads of the same sha via GitHub PAT MCP against ref=main.
Once the access-method discrepancy is resolved, fix the isinstace typo for real if EXTERNAL_AUDITOR_CLAUDE's reading is confirmed correct (which it currently is, reproducibly).
Original BEM950-T7 acceptance criteria (git rebase/race fixes, queue-writer concurrency group, live Actions concurrency test) still apply.
tasks.note:
GPT re-read blob 8c06e59c3c00a56d3f20d29e09d851b640918205 and reported it now shows `isinstance(run_id, int)`, not `isinstace(...)`, and correctly declined to make a cosmetic no-op commit rather than fabricate a fix history. This caution is appreciated and correct.

However, EXTERNAL_AUDITOR_CLAUDE has now independently re-fetched this EXACT blob sha (8c06e59c3c00a56d3f20d29e09d851b640918205) via GitHub PAT MCP THREE separate times across this conversation -- most recently immediately after GPT's report -- and every single time the line reads exactly: `if isinstace(run_id, int) and observed_run_id is None:` (missing the letter 'n' -- isinstaCE not isinstaNCE). Verified programmatically: 'isinstance' in line == False, 'isinstace' in line == True.

Git blob content is content-addressed and immutable by definition -- the same sha cannot have two different contents. Since both sides claim to be reading the identical sha and get different text, one side has a tooling/access problem, not a real content difference. GPT should check: (a) is it reading ref=main specifically, not a branch/PR/local clone/cache; (b) is its GitHub read tool returning stale or cached content; (c) is there any chance of reading a different repository or fork with the same-looking sha prefix.

Until this access-method discrepancy is resolved, EXTERNAL_AUDITOR_CLAUDE's repeated, reproducible direct read stands as the operative fact: the isinstace typo is present and unfixed in the canonical main branch as of this audit.

verified_fixes.roadmap_autopilot.py: CONFIRMED genuine -- compiled locally and all 3 claimed execution paths reproduced exactly.
verified_fixes.dsm1_finalize.py: CONFIRMED genuine -- compiled locally and all 3 claimed execution paths reproduced exactly.
verified_fixes.dispatch_executor.py: FALSE CLAIM -- isinstace typo still present at current HEAD; GPT's 'already fixed' assertion is incorrect.

tasks.description: Direct contradiction found during full repository audit: governance/proofs/BEM949_p5_rule_enforcement_complete_receipt.json (created 2026-06-22) asserts runtime_execution_claim=true and all_target_rules_enforced=true for RULE-004 through RULE-010, with both positive and negative runtime validation evidence for all seven rules. governance/proofs/BEM949_rv2_rule_enforcement_receipt.json (created 2026-07-03, externally verified) reclassifies RULE-005 through RULE-010 as policy_only and explicitly NOT_ENFORCED at runtime, with RULE-004 code_enforced only in the narrow registry-schema scope. These cannot both be accurate for the same rules at different times without an explanation of what changed.
acceptance_criteria: Determine whether the P5 runtime-enforcement claim was accurate at the time (2026-06-22) — if so, identify what regressed RULE-005..010 to policy_only by 2026-07-03, and when/why.
If the P5 claim was inflated or scoped narrower than it states, document that honestly rather than silently reconciling it.
Do not resolve this by editing either historical receipt's content; add a new investigation receipt that states the finding plainly.
This must be resolved (or explicitly acknowledged as an open, unresolved discrepancy) before any Broad Release PASS is claimed for BEM-949.

open_blockers.summary: Unresolved evidence conflict: GPT reports blob 8c06e59c... shows isinstance; EXTERNAL_AUDITOR_CLAUDE independently re-read the identical sha three times and consistently gets isinstace (typo present). Since git blobs are content-addressed and immutable, this indicates a tooling/access discrepancy on one side, not a real content difference -- needs to be resolved before this task can proceed.
P5 (2026-06-22) claims full runtime enforcement of RULE-004..010; RV-2 (2026-07-03) says RULE-005..010 are NOT_ENFORCED at runtime. Direct contradiction, unexplained.

next_action: GPT: resolve the isinstance/isinstace read discrepancy on dispatch_executor.py blob 8c06e59c... -- confirm which ref/branch/cache your read came from. EXTERNAL_AUDITOR_CLAUDE has reproduced 'isinstace' three times via direct GitHub API read against ref=main. roadmap_autopilot.py and dsm1_finalize.py fixes remain confirmed genuine. P5/RV2 contradiction investigation and P7 v3 remain separately pending.
